items.title: Subcomponents of reward processing in adolescent anhedonia
items.author: Ciara McCabe
items.content: Understanding the link between anhedonia and reward processing in adolescence can enhance mental health interventions specifically for young people. In this Review, Ma et al. examine the evidence on consummatory pleasure, anticipation, motivation and learning, and discuss real-time assessment of anhedonia and the reward cycle.

items.title: The psychological consequences of mental health awareness efforts
items.author: Carolina Guzman Holst
items.content: Mental health awareness campaigns aim to decrease stigma, increase help-seeking and improve mental health literacy. However, they might also negatively impact how individuals interpret, label and respond to mental health problems. In this Review, Foulkes et al. summarize evidence for the positive and negative psychological impacts of mental health awareness efforts.
